name: Brainfuck interpreter
task: Create /tmp/eval_bf/bf.py with a function bf(code, input_str='') that runs a Brainfuck program and returns the output as a string. Support all 8 commands: > < + - . , [ ]. The tape is unbounded to the right (initialize cells lazily), starts at position 0, and cells are unsigned 8-bit (wrap mod 256). Bracket matching must be O(n) preprocessed (don't re-scan on every loop iteration).

validation.script: import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_bf/bf.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); assert mod.bf('+++>++<[->+<]>.') == '\x05', 'simple add: 3+2=5'; assert mod.bf('+'*65 + '.') == 'A', 'output A via 65 increments'; hello = '++++++++[>++++[>++>+++>+++>+<<<<-]>+>+>->>+[<]<-]>>.>---.+++++++..+++.>>.<-.<.+++.------.--------.>>+.>++.'; out = mod.bf(hello); assert out == 'Hello World!\n', f'hello world got {out!r}'; assert mod.bf(',+.', 'A') == 'B', 'echo input + 1'; assert mod.bf('+'*256 + '.') == '\x00', 'wrap-around'; print('OK')
